description_long: This is a brass saxophone with a classic curved body, supported upright on a black tripod stand. The instrument features a gold-colored, metallic finish with a prominent bell at the lower end, which flares outward and has a smooth, reflective surface. The main tube is adorned with a series of metallic keys and pads, distributed along its length, with some side keys visible. The neck of the saxophone curves smoothly upward and is fitted with a black mouthpiece at the top. The stand is minimalistic, with three slender legs radiating outward for stability. No visible engravings or decorative etchings are present on the body, giving it a clean and professional appearance.
description: A gold brass saxophone with a curved neck, black mouthpiece, and metallic keys, standing on a black tripod stand. The bell is flared and reflective, with no visible engravings.
description_view_0: The saxophone is seen from a front-left angle, showing the full curve of the neck, the bell's flare, and the arrangement of keys along the body.
description_view_1: A direct front view, highlighting the circular bell opening and the alignment of the main tube and stand legs.
description_view_2: The saxophone is viewed from a front-right angle, displaying the opposite side of the keywork and the smooth curve of the neck.
description_view_3: A direct rear view, showing the back of the saxophone body, the stand, and minimal keywork detail.
attribute_bell_opening: The bell is circular, flared, and oriented slightly upward, with an estimated diameter of about 12 centimeters. The rim is smooth with no visible decorative features.
attribute_key_layout: The saxophone has a standard arrangement of metallic keys and pads distributed along the main tube, with several side keys visible. The keywork appears complete and typical for an alto or tenor saxophone.
attribute_neck_curve: The neck curves smoothly upward at an approximate 45-degree angle, with a continuous bend and no sharp angles. The neck appears detachable, as is standard for saxophones.
